title: What is ETL?
description: Learn the basics of Extract, Transform and Load process used in data warehousing.
content: ETL stands for Extract, Transform and Load. It is used to move data from source systems into a data warehouse. Data is extracted from different systems, transformed based on business rules and finally loaded into the target system.

title: What is ETL Testing?
description: Understand ETL testing concepts and data validation techniques.
content: ETL testing validates that data is correctly extracted, transformed and loaded into the target system. It ensures data accuracy, integrity and completeness throughout the ETL process.

title: What are the Main Phases of ETL?
description: Learn about Extract, Transform and Load phases with examples.
content: The three main phases of ETL are Extract, Transform and Load. Data is extracted from sources, transformed using business logic and loaded into the target warehouse.

title: What is a Staging Area?
description: Understand staging area concepts in ETL architecture.
content: A staging area is a temporary storage location where extracted data is stored before transformation and loading. It helps clean and validate data efficiently.

title: What is Data Transformation?
description: Learn how raw data is transformed into usable format.
content: Data transformation converts raw source data into meaningful and standardized data. It includes filtering, aggregation, calculations and format conversions.

title: What is Data Cleansing?
description: Understand duplicate removal and data quality improvement.
content: Data cleansing improves data quality by removing duplicates, correcting invalid values and handling null or inconsistent data.

title: What is a Fact Table?
description: Learn how measurable business data is stored in warehouses.
content: A fact table stores measurable business data such as sales, revenue and transactions. It usually contains foreign keys linked to dimension tables.

title: What is a Dimension Table?
description: Understand how dimension tables provide reporting context.
content: Dimension tables store descriptive attributes such as customer, product or location information. They help users analyze and filter fact table data.

title: What is a Surrogate Key?
description: Learn how surrogate keys uniquely identify warehouse records.
content: A surrogate key is an artificial key generated in the data warehouse to uniquely identify dimension table records independently of source system keys.

title: What is a Business Key?
description: Understand natural identifiers used in ETL systems.
content: A business key is a natural identifier from the source system such as customer ID or product ID used to track records.

title: What is Source to Target Mapping?
description: Learn how source data maps to target warehouse structures.
content: Source to target mapping defines how source fields are transformed and loaded into target fields in ETL systems.

title: What is Data Reconciliation?
description: Understand how source and target data are compared.
content: Data reconciliation compares source and target data to ensure records are loaded correctly without missing or extra records.

title: What is Data Integrity Testing?
description: Learn how relationships and consistency are validated.
content: Data integrity testing validates primary and foreign key relationships and ensures referential integrity between warehouse tables.

title: What is Duplicate Data Validation?
description: Understand duplicate checks in ETL pipelines.
content: Duplicate data validation identifies repeated records using SQL queries and business keys to ensure uniqueness.

title: What is NULL Validation?
description: Learn how ETL systems validate mandatory fields.
content: NULL validation checks whether mandatory fields contain missing values and ensures complete data loading.

title: What is Regression Testing in ETL?
description: Understand regression testing after ETL changes.
content: Regression testing verifies that existing ETL functionality continues working correctly after modifications or enhancements.

title: What is a Star Schema?
description: Learn star schema design in data warehouses.
content: Star schema contains one fact table connected directly to multiple dimension tables for efficient querying and reporting.

title: What is Snowflake Schema?
description: Understand normalized warehouse schema structures.
content: Snowflake schema is a normalized version of star schema where dimension tables are split into multiple related tables.

title: What is Full Load?
description: Learn how complete data loads work in ETL.
content: Full load transfers all records from the source to the target system and is commonly used during initial loading.

title: What is Incremental Load?
description: Understand incremental loading concepts and benefits.
content: Incremental load transfers only new or updated records after the initial full load to improve performance.

title: What is a Data Warehouse?
description: Learn about centralized repositories for analytics.
content: A data warehouse stores integrated historical data from multiple sources optimized for reporting and business intelligence.

title: What is a Data Mart?
description: Understand departmental subsets of data warehouses.
content: A data mart is a smaller subset of a data warehouse focused on a specific department such as sales or finance.

title: What is Referential Integrity?
description: Learn how relationships between tables are maintained.
content: Referential integrity ensures foreign keys correctly match primary keys and prevents orphan records.

title: What is Data Truncation?
description: Understand data loss caused by column length issues.
content: Data truncation occurs when incoming data exceeds the defined column size causing extra characters to be cut.

title: What is Data Aggregation?
description: Learn aggregation concepts used in reporting.
content: Data aggregation summarizes detailed data using operations such as SUM, COUNT and AVG for reporting purposes.

title: What is ETL Validation?
description: Understand validation of ETL processes and pipelines.
content: ETL validation verifies data movement, transformation rules and correctness between source and target systems.

title: What are Common ETL Defects?
description: Learn common ETL issues and defects.
content: Common ETL defects include missing records, duplicate data, incorrect transformations and datatype mismatches.

title: What is Data Profiling?
description: Understand analysis of source data structure and quality.
content: Data profiling analyzes data formats, patterns and inconsistencies to improve ETL design and data quality.

title: What is Batch Processing?
description: Learn how large datasets are processed together.
content: Batch processing handles large volumes of data together at scheduled intervals in ETL pipelines.

title: What is Real-Time ETL?
description: Understand streaming and real-time data integration.
content: Real-time ETL continuously processes data immediately after changes occur to support live analytics.

title: How Do You Validate Record Counts?
description: Learn record count validation techniques in ETL.
content: Record counts from source and target systems are compared using SQL COUNT queries to ensure completeness.

title: How Do You Validate Transformations?
description: Understand validation of transformation logic and rules.
content: Transformation validation compares transformed data against expected business logic using SQL queries and mappings.

title: How Do You Check Duplicates?
description: Learn duplicate detection techniques using SQL.
content: GROUP BY and COUNT queries are commonly used to identify duplicate records during ETL testing.

title: How Do You Validate Incremental Loads?
description: Understand validation of incremental loading logic.
content: Incremental load validation checks whether only new or modified records are loaded without duplication.

title: How Do You Test Large Data Loads?
description: Learn testing strategies for huge ETL datasets.
content: Large data loads are validated using sampling techniques, performance monitoring and SQL record count checks.

title: What is Data Lineage?
description: Understand how data flow is tracked in ETL systems.
content: Data lineage tracks where data originates, how it transforms and where it moves throughout ETL pipelines.

title: What is Metadata in ETL?
description: Learn how metadata describes structures and mappings.
content: Metadata describes table structures, transformation rules and mappings used by ETL tools and systems.

title: What is Data Warehouse Testing?
description: Understand validation of warehouse systems and reports.
content: Data warehouse testing validates warehouse data, ETL loading and schema relationships for accurate reporting.

title: What is Job Scheduling in ETL?
description: Learn how ETL jobs are automated and managed.
content: Job scheduling automates ETL workflow execution at predefined times while maintaining dependencies.

title: What is Partitioning?
description: Understand partitioning for large warehouse tables.
content: Partitioning divides large tables into smaller pieces to improve performance and reduce query scan time.

title: What is Indexing?
description: Learn how indexes improve ETL query performance.
content: Indexes improve query performance by reducing table scans and enabling faster data retrieval.

title: What is ETL Job Failure?
description: Understand ETL failures and troubleshooting approaches.
content: ETL job failure occurs when a workflow stops unexpectedly due to data issues, system failures or connectivity problems.

title: What is Error Handling in ETL?
description: Learn how ETL systems manage invalid data.
content: Error handling manages invalid records, logs failures and stores rejected data for troubleshooting and recovery.

title: What is Data Latency?
description: Understand delays between source and warehouse updates.
content: Data latency is the delay between source system updates and warehouse refreshes affecting reporting freshness.

title: What is Change Data Capture (CDC)?
description: Learn how CDC tracks changed records efficiently.
content: CDC identifies updated or new source records so only changed data is processed during incremental loading.

title: What is a Data Validation Rule?
description: Understand validation rules for accurate ETL data.
content: Data validation rules ensure proper formats, valid values and consistent business logic during ETL transformations.

title: What is an Audit Table in ETL?
description: Learn how ETL audit tables monitor workflows.
content: Audit tables track ETL job execution details such as record counts, timestamps and job status information.

title: What is Data Masking?
description: Understand protection of sensitive information in ETL.
content: Data masking hides confidential information such as SSN or credit card details in testing environments.

title: What is ETL Performance Tuning?
description: Learn optimization techniques for ETL performance.
content: ETL performance tuning improves efficiency by optimizing queries, transformations, indexing and parallel processing.

title: What are ETL Tools?
description: Understand popular ETL tools used in the industry.
content: ETL tools such as Informatica, Talend and DataStage automate extraction, transformation and loading processes efficiently.
